phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find the best falafel
phrases.text_local: Où trouver le meilleur falafel dans la rue des Rosiers ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo troo-VAY luh meh-YUHR fah-lah-FEL dahn lah roo day roh-ZYAY
phrases.cultural_note: L'As du Fallafel on Rue des Rosiers always has a line — it's worth it. Chez Hanna across the street is just as good with shorter waits. Get the 'spécial' with everything.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Place des Vosges
phrases.text_local: C'est par où la Place des Vosges ?
phrases.pronunciation: seh par OO lah PLAHS day VOHZH
phrases.cultural_note: Place des Vosges (1612) is Paris's oldest planned square. Victor Hugo lived at number 6, now a free museum. Sit on the grass in the center — it's one of the few Paris parks that allows it.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about vintage and boutique shopping
phrases.text_local: Où sont les meilleures boutiques vintage ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo sohn lay meh-YUHR boo-TEEK vahn-TAHZH
phrases.cultural_note: Rue de Sévigné and Rue des Francs-Bourgeois are great for independent boutiques. The Marais is open on Sundays — one of the few Paris neighborhoods where shops don't close.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the historic Jewish quarter
phrases.text_local: Où est le quartier juif historique ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh luh kar-TYAY zhoo-EEF ees-toh-REEK
phrases.cultural_note: Rue des Rosiers is the heart of the Pletzl (Jewish quarter). Visit the Shoah Memorial (free) and try the pastries at Florence Finkelsztajn bakery for authentic Eastern European treats.

phrases.text_local: Le musée Picasso est dans le Marais ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh moo-ZAY pee-kah-SOH eh dahn luh mah-REH
phrases.cultural_note: The Musée Picasso is in the Hôtel Salé on Rue de Thorigny. It houses over 5,000 works. Less crowded than the major museums and well worth the visit.
